questions.text: What sector does your organisation operate in?

options.label: Financial services
Healthcare and life sciences
Government and public sector
Energy and utilities
Other

questions.text: What best describes your role in AI investment decisions?

options.label: I own the budget and the outcome
I influence the decision but do not hold the budget
I am building the business case for someone above me
I am implementing what has already been decided

questions.text: Where is your organisation right now with AI?

options.label: Still exploring — no firm program yet
We have run pilots but nothing is in production
We have AI in production but it is not scaling
We are scaling AI but hitting unexpected walls

questions.text: If I asked your data team to trace your three most important business metrics back to their source systems today — could they do it?

options.label: Yes — we have documented end-to-end lineage
Partially — for some systems but not all
We could reconstruct it but it would take weeks
Honestly, no

questions.text: How many authoritative sources of truth exist for your organisation's core business data?

options.label: One — a single authoritative source that everyone trusts
Two or three — we manage the reconciliation ourselves
More than three — the answer depends on who you ask
We do not know

questions.text: Has a data quality or data governance issue ever delayed or killed an AI initiative in your organisation?

options.label: Yes — it was the primary reason the initiative failed or stalled
Yes — it was a contributing factor alongside other issues
Not yet, but it is a risk we are aware of
No — data has not been an issue for us

questions.text: Was the data quality issue known before the program started, or did it surface during implementation?

options.label: Known beforehand but deprioritised in favour of getting started
It surfaced during discovery and we adapted
It only became clear at deployment — too late to fix cheaply
It is still not fully understood

questions.text: Has your data infrastructure been formally assessed for AI workloads specifically — not just analytics or reporting?

options.label: Yes — assessed specifically for AI workloads within the last 12 months
Yes — but the assessment was for analytics, not AI specifically
No — but we are planning to do this
No — we have assumed it is ready based on our analytics capability

questions.text: When your AI model makes an incorrect or unexpected decision, who in your organisation can explain why — and point to the data that caused it?

options.label: Our data team can do this today — we have explainability tooling
We could reconstruct it with effort and time
We would have to make educated guesses
Nobody owns this accountability yet

questions.text: How would you honestly describe your organisation's data governance in practice — not on paper?

options.label: Active — it blocks bad decisions and is respected across the business
Documented — policies exist but are rarely enforced
Aspirational — we have the documents but not the behaviour
We do not have a formal data governance function

questions.text: What happened to the most significant AI initiative your organisation has run?

options.label: It went to production and is delivering the value that was promised
It delivered insight but stalled when we tried to scale it
It was quietly shelved
We have not run a significant AI initiative yet

questions.text: What do you believe is your organisation's single biggest barrier to getting AI into production?

options.label: Our data is not in a state that would support AI reliably
We do not have the internal skills to build or operate AI systems
We cannot get executive commitment or budget allocation
We do not have clearly defined use cases with measurable outcomes
Budget has not been allocated

questions.text: Where specifically are the walls you are hitting?

options.label: Data quality is degrading as volume and variety increase
Our governance processes cannot keep pace with deployment speed
Model performance is below expectation in production conditions
Business adoption is low despite technical delivery
The cost of running AI at scale is exceeding the value it delivers

questions.text: What was the primary reason given for shelving the initiative?

options.label: Data was not ready to support the use case
Cost overran the business case
Business priorities shifted and the sponsor moved on
Results did not justify continuation
The reason was never formally documented or discussed

questions.text: Is it delivering the business value that was originally promised in the business case?

options.label: Yes — it is exceeding the original projections
Yes — roughly in line with what was promised
Partially — some value but below what was sold to the business
No — it is underperforming and we are not sure why

questions.text: Who owns the business outcome of your AI program — not the technology, the outcome?

options.label: A named executive with personal accountability for delivery
A steering committee with shared accountability
The IT or data team
Ownership is unclear or contested

questions.text: When AI investment decisions need to be made, what typically happens in practice?

options.label: Decisions get escalated and delayed — no clear authority exists
Different teams make competing or conflicting decisions
Decisions default to vendor recommendations by default
Decisions simply do not get made in a reasonable timeframe

questions.text: How long does a material change to your core data architecture typically take to approve and implement?

options.label: Days — we move quickly and have clear approval authority
Weeks — there is a governance process but it is manageable
Months — approvals are slow and cross-departmental
It has never been done cleanly — it always involves technical debt

questions.text: What is driving urgency around AI in your organisation right now?

options.label: Competitive pressure — we are falling behind peers
Board or regulatory mandate — we have been directed to act
Internal cost reduction targets tied to AI automation
An internal champion is pushing it without a formal mandate
There is no active urgency yet

questions.text: Has budget been formally allocated for your AI program?

options.label: Yes — approved and available now
Not yet — we are building the business case
Budget was allocated but has since been reduced or frozen
No budget discussion has started

riskFlagRules.title: No documented data lineage
riskFlagRules.body: No documented data lineage exists. AI models built on this foundation cannot be trusted or explained.

riskFlagRules.title: Prior AI failure with unidentified root cause
riskFlagRules.body: A prior AI initiative failed due to data quality, and the root cause has not been formally identified.

riskFlagRules.title: No explainability accountability
riskFlagRules.body: No individual or team owns explainability for AI decisions. This is a regulatory liability in regulated sectors.

riskFlagRules.title: AI decisions default to vendor
riskFlagRules.body: AI investment decisions are defaulting to vendor recommendations without internal accountability.

riskFlagRules.title: Previous initiative shelved without post-mortem
riskFlagRules.body: A previous AI initiative was shelved without a documented post-mortem. The failure mode is likely to recur.

riskFlagRules.title: Governance exists on paper only
riskFlagRules.body: Data governance exists on paper but is not enforced in practice.

priorityTriggers.reason: Board or regulatory mandate — buyer is under board or regulator timeline pressure regardless of org maturity. Highest-priority outreach.
Budget approved and available — decision-maker has money and authority. High-priority outreach.
